feedback.text: The initiative should also address the problem of consumers losing access to paid digital products. For example, people often buy video games or apps and later find them removed or disabled by the provider, with no refund, replacement, or way to keep using the product. These are not subscriptions or services, but one-time purchases. Consumers expect to keep access, as they would with physical goods. Right now, there are no clear protections when these products are taken away. I ask the EU to include this issue in the review. Sellers should be required to offer alternatives such as offline versions, refunds, or continued access when a paid digital product becomes unavailable. Thank you for considering this.
